items.title: CMS issues guidance on upcoming limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS said states must implement new statutory limits on when they can claim federal matching funds (FFP) for Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens starting 2026-10-01. CMS noted exceptions, including continued federal matching for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the changes do not apply to the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: Medicaid.gov posts Working Families Tax Cut implementation hub (includes new guidance links for eligibility reforms)
items.summary: CMS posted a dedicated page to centralize guidance and technical assistance for Medicaid/CHIP changes in Public Law 119-21 (the "Working Families Tax Cut"). The page highlights an "Alien Medicaid Eligibility" item posted 2026-04-08 (section 71109) and other Medicaid eligibility redetermination resources posted in March and April 2026.

items.title: USDA posts tracker of SNAP food restriction waivers (multiple 2026 implementation dates)
items.summary: USDA published a table of states with approved SNAP food restriction waivers, including target implementation dates such as Florida (2026-04-20) and Colorado (2026-04-30). The waivers generally restrict purchases of items like soda, candy, energy drinks, and other specified products depending on the state.

items.title: Pennsylvania extends 2025-26 LIHEAP application season to May 8, 2026
items.summary: Pennsylvania DHS extended the 2025-26 LIHEAP season from April 10 to May 8, 2026, citing adequate funding to continue the season after a delayed opening due to a federal shutdown. DHS reported LIHEAP has helped more than 233,000 households with an average $282 cash benefit and nearly 83,000 households with an average $558 crisis benefit so far this season.

items.title: HUD rescinds streamlined waiver process meant to expedite voucher admissions for people experiencing homelessness
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-04 rescinds prior guidance (Notice PIH 2024-17) that described a streamlined review process for regulatory waivers to expedite admission to Housing Choice Vouchers (HCV), Project-based Vouchers (PBV), and public housing for people experiencing homelessness. HUD says waivers already approved or granted under the prior process remain valid until their stated expiration.

items.title: HUD releases CY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher administrative fee rate guidance (including proration and Mainstream voucher change)
items.summary: HUD said CY 2026 HCV administrative fees will be paid based on units leased as of the first day of each month (from Voucher Management System data), with Column A rates applying to the first 7,200 unit-months and Column B to additional unit-months. HUD also said advanced administrative fees for January through March 2026 were at 88% proration, and starting in CY 2026 renewal funding for Mainstream vouchers will be included with the regular HCV program.

items.title: HUD sets April 14, 2026 deadline for PHAs to apply for $10 million in additional HUD-VASH administrative fees
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-01 (Revised) announces $10 million in additional administrative fee funding for public housing agencies administering HUD-VASH vouchers. HUD says applications must be received no later than midnight on April 14, 2026 (in the PHA’s time zone).

items.title: IRS opens 2026 filing season; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2, 2026 for many early filers
items.summary: The IRS said the federal filing deadline for 2025 tax returns is April 15, 2026. The agency also said most refunds claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) should be available by March 2, 2026 for taxpayers who choose direct deposit and have no other issues, and that "Where’s My Refund?" will provide projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: SSA posts 2026 SSI federal payment amounts after 2.8% COLA
items.summary: SSA said the 2.8% COLA becomes effective in January 2026 and increases maximum federal SSI payment amounts. The monthly maximum federal SSI amounts for 2026 are $994 for an eligible individual, $1,491 for an eligible couple, and $498 for an essential person.

items.title: USDA reminds WIC agencies: by April 2026, authorize at least one non-fresh form of fruits/vegetables for the cash-value benefit
items.summary: USDA FNS said recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to allow participants to buy at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. FNS also published FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit amounts, including $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants.
